mnemonics: A fork in the blockchain road: one path continues the old rules (soft fork), the other creates a new path with new rules (hard fork).
keyPoints: A soft fork is a backward-compatible upgrade where only previously valid blocks become invalid.
A hard fork is a non-backward-compatible upgrade that creates a permanent divergence from the previous version of the blockchain.
Forks can be planned (upgrades) or contentious (splits in the community).
commonMistakes: Thinking a soft fork doesn't create a split chain (it can, but the network typically follows the new rules).
Believing a hard fork always results in two valuable coins (the new chain often lacks support and value).
